title: SENIOR FARM DIRECT NUTRITION PROGRAM - 211info
text: OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION SENIOR FARM DIRECT NUTRITION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides eligible seniors with vouchers that can be used at participating farm stands and markets to purchase locally grown fresh fruits and vegetables. Vouchers can be used from June 1 to November 30. 800 NE Oregon Street Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: To be eligible for the program, seniors must meet ALL of the following criteria: -Be at least 62 years old by April 1 of the current year -Be receiving either SNAP benefits and/or Medicaid on April 1 of the current year -Have income below 138% of the Federal Poverty Level in the current year -Be living in a situation where food is not provided for them Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email - (866) 299-3562 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Toll-free (866) 299-3562 Senior Farm Direct Nutrition Program Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Participation is by invitation only. Eligible seniors are sent an invitation in the mail in late April. Call for information or visit website for locations of farm stands and farmers markets. Voicemail option is only available during service hours and if specialists are available. Accommodations are made upon request by contacting the market or stand. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Food Vouchers * Older Adults Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - ALERT IMMUNIZATION INFORMATION SYSTEM COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - OREGON SCREENWISE PROGRAM CONSUMER REGULATION - BIRTH CERTIFICATES CONSUMER REGULATION - OREGON VITAL RECORDS CONSUMER REGULATION - SENIOR FARM DIRECT APPLICATION FOR FARMERS & FARM STANDS DISASTER SERVICES - STATE EMERGENCY REGISTRY OF VOLUNTEERS IN OREGON DISASTER SERVICES - WINTER WEATHER ADVISORIES ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AND IMPROVEMENT - CLIMATE CHANGE AND HEALTH FOOD - WIC FARM DIRECT NUTRITION PROGRAM HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - BREAST AND CERVICAL CANCER TREATMENT HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OREGON IMMUNIZATION PROGRAM HUMAN REPRODUCTION - A SAFE PLACE FOR NEWBORNS HUMAN REPRODUCTION - REPRODUCTIVE AND SEXUAL HEALTH HUMAN REPRODUCTION - STATE OF OREGON CONDOM DELIVERY PROGRAM INFORMATION SERVICES - DEATH WITH DIGNITY ACT MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - PSILOCYBIN SERVICES PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - OREGON PRESCRIPTION DRUG PROGRAM ARRAYRX PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - OREGON WIC ADMINISTRATION PUBLIC HEALTH - COMMUNICABLE DISEASE INFORMATION PUBLIC HEALTH - ENVIRONMENTAL PUBLIC HEALTH PUBLIC HEALTH - LOCAL PUBLIC HEALTH AUTHORITY DIRECTORY PUBLIC SAFETY - OHA DRINKING WATER DATA ONLINE REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - EARLY HEARING DETECTION AND INTERVENTION SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - OREGON MEDICAL MARIJUANA PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - VACCINES FOR CHILDREN SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - OREGON HOPELINE SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - TOBACCO QUIT LINE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment.
